DAYDREAM | Air Becomes Art - Balloon Museum
description:
DAYDREAM - Air Becomes Art
Created by the globally renowned Balloon Museum, which has welcomed over 8 million visitors worldwide, DAYDREAM - Air Becomes Art is the inaugural exhibition of its first permanent U.S. flagship, located in New York City’s iconic Tin Building at the Seaport.
Featuring immersive and interactive installations by internationally acclaimed artists including Marina Abramović, Martin Creed, Alex Schweder, Thom Kubli, Karina Smigla-Bobinski, Valerio Berruti, and many others, the exhibition invites visitors to move beyond observation and become active participants in the artistic experience.
Inspired by the moment when everyday reality gives way to imagination, DAYDREAM transforms spaces into extraordinary environments where air, light, sound, and movement become powerful creative languages. Through playful interaction, exploration, and sensory engagement, visitors are encouraged to discover new perspectives and experience art in unexpected ways.
Located in the heart of New York City’s historic Seaport district, DAYDREAM - Air Becomes Art  offers a unique journey into a world where reality becomes more vivid, immersive, and full of wonder for visitors of all ages.

name: Rock Never Dies
description: Click HERE for all event datesFall in Love with Rock All Over AgainInside the legendary Hard Rock Cafe in Times Square, discover an experience where music isnt just heard but felt.Rock Never Dies is a one-of-a-kind immersive theatrical event from American Immersion Theater that is part concert, part love story, and fully a magical journey. This groundbreaking production invites you to dine, drink, and fall headfirst into the soul of rock and roll.Enjoy a full three-course meal inside the Magical Mystery Museum of Hard Rock, a place where music has power, memories come alive, and one life is changed forever every night. Its an experience that inspires you to keep dreaming and march to the beat of your own drum.At the heart of the story is Caroline, a young artist whos lost hope and heart. As legendary music icons come to life around her, shes forced to confront love, loss, and the courage to take the stage again. Along the way, you wont just watch the story, but be part of it.Created by award-winning, immersive visionary Scott Cramton, often called the Willy Wonka of Immersive Theater, this joyful, high-energy spectacle blends iconic rock anthems with romance, comedy, and electrifying audience interaction.Whether youre on a date, celebrating something special, or just chasing that feeling music used to give you, this is your night.All it takesis one song,one moment,and a second chanceto change everything.During Rock Never Dies, guests will indulge in a 3-course meal created by Certified Executive Chef David DeLeon of Hard Rock International. This decadent meal includes a Blueberry Basil Green Salad for a starter, your choice of a Petit Grilled Sirloin and Shrimp, Seared Brown Butter Chicken and Grilled Salmon, a Classic Smash Burger, or Pasta Primavera for an entree, and a Cheesecake with fresh berries for dessert. *Menu is subject to change based on ingredient availability.

name: Gerry Gibbs Quintet ft. Gary Bartz, Randy Brecker, Buster Williams, Cyrus Chestnut
description: Composer, Arranger, Bandleader Gerry Gibbs presents the 2026 edition of The Dream Band for five nights at Birdland Jazz Club featuring NEA Jazz Masters saxophonist Gary Bartz, trumpeter Randy Brecker, bassist Buster Williams, with acclaimed pianist Cyrus Chestnut.With 17 solo albums and multiple GRAMMY nominations, drummer Gerry Gibbs has performed, toured or recorded with a whos who in the music world such as Alice Coltrane, McCoy Tyner, Chick Corea, Flying Lotus, Kamasi Washington, Thundercat, Sarah Vaughn, Stanley Clarke, Brad Mehldau, Mike Stern, Joe Henderson, Donald Byrd, Parliament Funkadelics, Joey Defransesco, Ornette Coleman, Woody Shaw, Sam Rivers, Clark Terry, James Moody, Dewey Redman and many more. NEA Jazz Master and legendary alto saxophonist Gary Bartz has worked with jazz icons including Miles Davis, Max Roach, Charles Mingus, and Art Blakey.Celebrated seven-time GRAMMY Award winner and NEA Jazz Master trumpeter Randy Brecker was a foundational figure in jazz-fusion. He is widely recognized for his work with the Brecker Brothers and his extensive contributions to the recordings of artists ranging from Frank Sinatra to Parliament-Funkadelic.Highly acclaimed jazz pianist and composer Cyrus Chestnut signed to Atlantic Records and accompanied legends Betty Carter, Jimmy Heath, and James Moody on his way to being called, the best jazz pianist of his generation, by Time Magazine.Legendary bassist and NEA Jazz Master Buster Williams has been at the forefront of the jazz scene since the 1960s when he performed and recorded with Miles Davis, Herbie Hancock, and Nancy Wilson.

name: Danny Tobias Quartet
description: Trenton native Danny Tobias brings beautiful shape, swing and individuality to his jazz performances. Playing professionally since his teens, and a mentee of Tony DiNicola and Kenny Davern, hes best known for his artful improvisation. Dannys skills and repertoire embrace a variety of styles, although he is strongly influenced by Louis Armstrong, Ruby Braff, Buck Clayton and Chet Baker. Danny has enjoyed performing and recording with prolific artists such as Warren Vach, Scott Robinson, Rossano Sportiello, Mark Shane, Vince Giordano, David Ostwald, Gordon Webster, Terry Waldo, Harry Allen, Dan Block, Jon-Erik Kellso, Kevin Dorn, the Midiri Brothers, Marty Grosz and Howard Alden. He can be seen performing in New York City at Birdland (often with David Ostwalds Louis Armstrong Eternity Band), Mezzrow, the Ear Inn, and Zinc Bar, to name a few, and at jazz festivals and jazz parties across the USA and Canada. Danny has recorded a number of albums as a leader. His most recent album Silver Linings was recorded on Ride Symbol Records. A veteran educator, Danny also mentors musicians of all ages, encouraging them to reach the heights of their skill and creativity. Lessons are available in person, or remotely.

name: Lil Mo Mozzarella
description: Straight outta Brooklyn and raised in Queens, Lil Mo Mozzarella is a born entertainer part food critic, part street philosopher, and full blown comedian now taking the nation by storm on his WANTED Comedy Tour. He first blew up during the pandemic, making waves and mouths water with his raw, hilarious food reviews. From neighborhood bakeries and mom and pop delis to slice joints and soul food staples, Lil Mo hit over 500 small businesses between 2020 and 2022 shouting out the culture, the community, and every mouthwatering bite along the way.

name: The Odyssey
description: The Odyssey 3 Hours R Action & Fantasy Get your tickets online in advance, or skip the Ticketmaster fees and get them at the door! Directed By: Christopher Nolan Written By: Christopher Nolan & Homer Stars: Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway After the Trojan War, Odysseus faces a dangerous voyage back to Ithaca, meeting creatures like the Cyclops Polyphemus, Sirens, and Calypso along the way.

name: Musical: Annie
description: Winner of 7 Tony Awards including Best Musical! With equal measures of pluck and positivity, little orphan Annie charms everyones hearts despite a next to nothing start in 1930s New York City. She is determined to find the parents who abandoned her years ago on the doorstep of a New York City Orphanage that is run by the cruel, embittered Miss Hannigan. With the help of the other girls in the Orphanage, Annie escapes to the wondrous world of NYC. In adventure after fun filled adventure, Annie foils Miss Hannigans evil machinations... and even befriends President Franklin Delano Roosevelt! She finds a new home and family in billionaire, Oliver Warbucks, his personal secretary, Grace Farrell, and a lovable mutt named Sandy. Table Seating tickets include a cocktail server (credit card required) before the show and during intermission! The Milford Theater Bar will be open one hour before the show, serving wine, beer, cocktails and concessions!

name: Greg Hawkes with Eddie Japan Performing the Music of The Cars
description: As the keyboardist for the legendary Boston band and Rock s New Wave. Pioneering and pushing the limits of technology and sequencing in the late 70s and through the 80s, Greg cemented the synthesizers place in rock and pop music, and his contribution has influenced countless artists since. Along with achieving Rock & Roll Hall of Fame status and massive commercial success with The Cars, Greg has been a sought-after session and touring musician, and has worked with the likes of Paul McCartney, Todd Rundgren, and The Turtles.

name: Upcycling Stencil Workshop
description:
Looking for an art workshop for practical art you’ll use in daily life? 

Break the rules! We are offering a new workshop where you can create your own street-art inspired masterpiece using authentic stencil techniques, on any and as many materials as you like. Bring your own jacket, tote bag, sneakers, canvas, pants, or just use one of our provided T-shirts. Feeling expressive? Stencil directly onto your body with skin-safe materials. 

No experience required. Leave with wearable art that is 100% unique.


$20 per person for the class ($30 if you want to use a provided shirt)
Materials provided. T-shirts included while supplies last.

name: Banksy Museum NY - REBEL STENCIL WORKSHOP
description:
Make your own Banksy-inspired art with our popular all ages stenciling workshop hosted right here at the museum! Create pieces that are creative, and totally unique with no experience required. All materials provided. 

After class, the museum is yours! Entry is included with each ticket. Wander, snap photos, and soak up the creativity as long as you like!

1–3 PM Stencil Class • Stay as long as you like 
Spots fill fast — grab yours now and make your weekend a work of art!

NOW ALSO OFFERING: Weekend Guided Tour and Stencil Class Combo! Make it a full day with both our Stencil Class and our Guided Tour plus entrance to the museum!

name: MOCACREATE: Playful Pastels with Teaching Artist Yu Rong
description:
Create your own vibrant pastel art inspired by Ed Young's iconic soft pastel illustrations. Using stencils of Ed Young's beloved characters, or one of your own, layer soft pastels and scribble and smudge your bright world to reality! Design a unique setting around your character, whether it's a blooming garden, a bustling city street, or a fantastical dreamscape. 

On July 25th only,  don’t miss Little Red Riding Hood, a Chinese opera that reimagines a classic tale through a traditional art form. RSVP for MOCACREATE and receive an exclusive discount code! 

Paint, build, craft, and collage with us! Inspired by the museum’s exhibitions and local artists, MOCACREATE explores different themes every month. MOCACREATE workshops are designed with families in mind, but all are welcome!

Free with admission! RSVP appreciated. MOCA Members should enter their promo code to access complimentary tickets. NYC residents can purchase pay-what-you wish tickets online or with our Visitor Services team. 

 General Admission

Adults: $15
Seniors (over 55): $10
Students (with valid ID): $10
Visitors with disabilities: $10

Member Admission: FREE
Not a MOCA member yet? Join MyMOCA now to receive exclusive member benefits, special discounts and offerings, and “First to Know!” announcements about MOCA’s exhibitions and public programs.

Pay-What-You-Wish Admission
NYC Residents (Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, The Bronx, Staten Island)
Accepted Residency Verification:

New York State driver’s license
New York State ID card
IDNYC
New York library card
Current bill or statement with a New York State address

Pay-what-you-wish admission can only be purchased in person with a residency verification.

Complimentary Admission and Passes
Eligible visitors must present valid ID, membership card, or pass upon arrival. Complimentary admission is available to:

Children under 12
Caregivers accompanying visitors with disabilities
Active-duty military personnel and families (with valid military ID)
Health care workers (with valid employee ID)
Cool Culture Family Pass holders (up to 5 family members per visit)
Culture Pass holders (New York, Brooklyn, Queens Public Libraries, with reservation)
SNAP/EBT cardholders
American Alliance of Museums (AAM) members
International Council of Museums (ICOM) members
Museums Council of New York City members
Press and media (with valid press ID)
Corporate Partners (employees with valid corporate ID)
Reciprocal members from participating institutions
Bronx Museum Pass holders
Staff of other museums and cultural organizations (with valid employee ID)

Yu Rong is a painter and product designer. She was born in Sichuan and currently lives in New York City, where she is a teaching artist at the Hope Garden Center and at MOCA. She received her BFA in Product Design and MFA in Painting at Parsons School of Design and NYSS, but has been practicing art ever since she was a child. You can learn more about her here: www.yurong.work!


MOCACREATE is supported, in part, by public funds from the New York City Department of Cultural Affairs, in partnership with the City Council and the New York State Council on the Arts under Governor Kathy Hochul’s Regional Economic Development Council Initiative.

name: Museum of Chinese in America General Admission
description:
The Museum of Chinese in America (MOCA) is dedicated to preserving, illuminating, and celebrating the rich history and contributions of Chinese in America. By fostering understanding and greater recognition of our experiences, we endeavor to inspire and connect all Americans.

Hours

Monday – Tuesday: CLOSED TO GENERAL PUBLIC. Guided Group Tours ONLY. Please see our Group Tours page for more information.
Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Thursday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Friday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Saturday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM

Kindly note the Museum is closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day.

General Admission

Adults: $15
Seniors (55 and over): $10
Students (with valid student ID): $10
Visitors with disabilities: $10

Member Admission: FREE
Not a MOCA member yet? Join MyMOCA now to receive exclusive member benefits, special discounts and offerings, and "First to Know!" announcements about MOCA's exhibitions and public programs. Click Here to become a MyMOCA Member!

Complimentary Admission and Passes
Eligible visitors must present valid ID, membership card, or pass upon arrival. Complimentary admission is available to:

Children under 12
Caregivers accompanying visitors with disabilities
Active-duty military personnel and families (with valid military ID)
Health care workers (with valid employee ID)
Cool Culture Family Pass holders (up to 5 family members per visit)
Culture Pass holders (New York, Brooklyn, Queens Public Libraries, with reservation)
SNAP/EBT cardholders
American Alliance of Museums (AAM) members
International Council of Museums (ICOM) members
Museums Council of New York City members
Press and media (with valid press ID)
Corporate Partners (employees with valid corporate ID)
Reciprocal members from participating institutions
Bronx Museum Pass holders
Staff of other museums and cultural organizations (with valid employee ID)

Group Visits and Tours
Reservations are required for all groups of 8 or more planning to visit the Museum, as well as third-party guided tours of any size. Groups may request either a guided visit, led by a trained Museum Educator, or a self-guided visit, which allows you to lead your own group through the Museum. Please see our Group Tours page for more information. Title I NYC public schools or other groups with limited resources may qualify for discounted rates. Please ask for details when you request a reservation.

Ticketed Events
Tickets purchased for MOCA public programs and special events include same-day general admission, providing visitors the opportunity to explore the museum before or after their event.

For the latest information on MOCA’s admission policy, please visit Plan Your Visit.

name: MOCA PERFORMS – Little Red Riding Hood, A Chinese Opera
description:
Continuing more than a century of Chinese opera’s legacy in the United States, this program reimagines a classic tale through the lens of Chinese opera—inviting older generations to reminisce and younger Asian Americans to discover the beauty of this timeless art form. Presented in a nontraditional space, the performance pays homage to Chinese opera’s arrival in America during the mid-19th century. Through this world-renowned tale, audiences will experience acrobatics, martial arts, and elaborate costumes in a celebration of the nation’s rich cultural diversity and this enduring artistic tradition. This program is in partnership with Chinese Theater Works.

CREATIVE TEAM
Richard Chang
Suosen Lyu
Qi Zhang
Jing Shan
Huixin Bai
Xing Wang, Music Director
Kuang-Yu Fong, Playwright and Director

This event forms part of Carnegie Hall’s United in Sound: America at 250 festival.
ABOUT Chinese Theater Works
Chinese Theatre Works (CTW) was created in 2001 out of the merger of two non-profit institutions with long histories of bringing traditional and innovative, contemporary Chinese performing arts to local New York City, national and international audiences—The Gold Mountain Institute for Traditional Shadow Theater (GMI) and Chinese Theatre Workshop.

name: Are You Now or Have You Ever Been
A Brighter Summer Day (1991)
description: Taipei, 1960. Fourteen-year-old Xiao Sir drifts toward trouble after being transferred to a night school. Edward Yang based the film on a real incident that shook Taiwan during his youth, a first case in the nations history. The title comes from a line in the 1960 Elvis Presley rendition of the song Are You Lonesome Tonight?

name: Ghost in the Shell (1995) *Midnight Screening*
description: In the year 2029, Major Motoko Kusanagi is hunting a mysterious brain-hacker known as the Puppet Master, who has intervened in politics through the vulnerabilities of cybernetics. Mamoru Oshiis landmark cyberpunk film left a lasting impression on science fiction cinema around the world, influencing works from The Matrix to contemporary video games.

name: Chronicle of a Summer (1961)
description: Are you happy? With that question, filmmakers Jean Rouch and Edgar Morin set out to document everyday life in Paris. A foundational work of cinma vrit, the film explores the truths that can be captured in the medium of film. In doing so, a timeless human spirit is depicted in all its long and mundane suffering.
